text:
Market today: Mood is cautious-to-risk-off. The real driver for your names is headline #9 — AI hardware hitting a wall. Optical and memory weakness bleeds directly into chip and semiconductor exposure. Tariff noise on digital services adds another overhang on tech multiples.

Your holdings: AVGO is the one demanding attention right now — your largest position, down 15.5%, on margin. AI hardware headwinds hit it directly. PLTR is down 13.4% and a digital-services tariff target could reprice growth names fast. SPAL is down 17.2% — it's a 2x leveraged ETF that decays daily; that loss compounds quickly and margin amplifies every tick lower. SPCX is brand-new, very volatile, barely any price history — treat it as a hand grenade, not a position.

Watch: 1) Any follow-through selling in semis/AI hardware names — SMH is your only green chip name, protect that 10% gain. 2) Iran/Hormuz escalation — oil spike would rotate money away from tech fast.

Bottom line: Your two biggest losers (AVGO, PLTR) are getting hit by the exact headline dominating today — cut size before the stop does it for you.
